steps.n: This is the basic form of the word that means 'when' in a general sense

steps.m: when (referring to a future time and introducing the condition جَآءَ 'comes')
steps.n: Adding ا at the end makes it specifically about future time. In this ayah, it introduces what will happen when Allah's help and victory come.
Connected the root letters ج-ي-أ with the vowel marks َ (fatha) to make the past tense verb 'he came'
Changed جَيَأَ to جَاءَ by adding alif after ج and changing weak letters
Added long alif to show extended duration of coming
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to change from action to noun
This word is the subject (doer) of the verb جَآءَ (came). The ُ at the end shows it's doing the action of coming.
Added ال and merged it with the word, changing إ to ل to create the unique name Allah
Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added kasra at end to show possession

steps.m: to open or make a way through something
victory/conquest (the act of opening the way)
steps.n: Root letters were given vowels to make a noun meaning 'victory' instead of the basic verb 'to open'

steps.m: the victory/conquest (referring to a specific victory)
steps.n: Adding ال and ُ makes this refer to the specific victory Allah is mentioning, not just any victory

steps.m: and the victory/conquest (connected to نَصْرُ ٱللَّهِ - Allah's help)
steps.n: Adding و connects this to 'Allah's help' mentioned just before it, showing these two things come together

steps.m: to see with eyes or mind

steps.n: Changed the root letters into past tense 'he saw' by adding vowels and using ى at the end
The ى changes back to يْ before adding endings
Added تَ to show you (masculine) did the action. This word is connected to يَدْخُلُونَ (they enter) in this ayah - you saw them entering.

steps.m: and you saw (the subject of يَدْخُلُونَ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with what came before
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense

steps.m: people (those who are social/friendly with each other)
steps.n: Changed from a verb meaning 'to be friendly' to a plural noun meaning 'people' who live together in society
Added 'al' (the) at the beginning, which made the first letter double to connect smoothly
Added fatha at end to show this is the object being seen in رَأَيْتَ (you saw)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb
Changes from past tense 'he entered' to present tense 'enter' by changing middle vowel to 'u' sound
Adds present tense marker to make it a present tense verb

steps.m: they enter (referring to people entering اللَّهِ دِينِ - Allah's religion)
steps.n: Adds masculine plural subject marker to indicate multiple doers
This is a preposition that shows people are entering into Allah's religion. It connects the verb يَدْخُلُونَ (they enter) with دِينِ (religion).
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to judge, to follow a way

steps.n: The kasra vowel transforms the root into a noun denoting an abstract concept/system
The kasra shows this word belongs to or is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) in this ayah
Added emphasis by doubling the ل
Added ِ at the end to show this connects to the word دِينِ (religion) before it, showing it's Allah's religion
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning one group
Changed to broken plural pattern by adding أَ at start and ا after و to make it mean many groups

steps.m: in groups (describing how people are entering)
steps.n: Added tanween fatah at the end to show this describes how people are entering Allah's religion - they are entering in groups
Added vowels to make past tense 'he' form - means someone praised/glorified
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger, like saying 'really glorified'
Changed to command form by removing final vowel and adding sukun
Added فَ to connect this command with what came before it
Changed the basic idea of praising into a noun meaning 'praise' itself
Added ِ to show this connects to 'your Lord' (رَبِّكَ) that comes after it
Added بِ at the beginning to show this praise is being used as a tool or method, connecting to the command فَسَبِّحْ (so glorify) that comes before it

steps.m: to care for and help grow

steps.n: Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he cared for'

steps.m: one who cares for others completely
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) shows this person does a lot of caring for others
Added kasra (i sound) to show this means 'my Lord' or 'Lord of' something
The كَ at the end means 'your', showing that the Lord belongs to you (the person being spoken to). This word is connected to حَمْدِ (praise) to show whose praise it is.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or requesting forgiveness
Changed from 'he sought' to 'you seek!' by changing the vowels to make it a command to one person
Added هُ to show the object (Him/Allah) whose forgiveness is being sought

steps.m: and seek His forgiveness! (command after فَسَبِّحْ)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this command with the previous command (فَسَبِّحْ - glorify)
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English
Added هُ at the end to show who or what is being talked about (he/it)
Added the small و mark (ۥ) which helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning
Added short vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it mean 'he was' in the past tense
Added alif after first letter because و changes to ا in hollow verbs
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb. The و changes to ا because it's between two vowels.
The doubled و and added ا make this an intensive form meaning someone who does this action a lot

steps.m: one who constantly accepts repentance (describing كَانَ - He was)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word describes كَانَ (He was) in the ayah
